reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) stopped extending new loans on March 11, 2024. Because loans were limited to a term of up to one year, all outstanding loans matured and were repaid by March 11, 2025. This is confirmed by FRED data showing the series (H41RESPPALDKNWW) hitting 0.0 in mid-March 2025 and remaining precisely at 0.0 every week through late January 2026. Because the program is closed, no new loans can be issued. The probability of the outstanding balance increasing above 0.0 by February 8 or March 3, 2026, is therefore extremely low, reflecting only the tail risk of the Federal Reserve suddenly reopening the exact same program within the next month due to an unforeseen, acute banking crisis. Uncertainty marginally increases for the later date.
